observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer/Inventory Committee to provide complete information in the RPCPPE and
to reconcile the same with the records of the Accounting Office.
We also recommended to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan
to the Audit Team at least 10 days before the scheduled start of inventory taking
activities in adopting COA Circular 2020-006 relative to one time cleansing of PPE
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
1. Municipal Engineer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with documents that
will determine when the aforementioned infrastructure assets became
operational as to its intended use; and
2. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments on the affected
accounts and take up the necessary depreciation expense for these assets.

We recommended that the BAC Chairperson to submit to the Audit Team for evaluation the written explanation/justification for observing strict compliance with the GPPB guidelines on mandatory posting of all emergency procurements of the Municipality. Henceforth, comply with the existing rules and regulations relative to this.
observations.managementAction: emergency procurements were already posted in the GPPB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: ● Municipal Treasurer/Inventory Committee and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to determine the cause of discrepancy of their records and take appropriate action for the result of reconciliation; ● Municipal Treasurer/Inventory Committee to provide/complete/correct the information in the RPCPPE such as the conditions, whereabouts, description, property number, unit of measure and other relevant information relative to the property and prepare a separate report for its unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and dispose the same following the guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and ● OIC-Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation for all depreciable PPE.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant were able to determine the discrepancy of their records and take appropriate action for the result of reconciliation. The Municipal Treasurer/Inventory Committee was able to provide/complete/correct the information in the RPCPPE such as the conditions, whereabouts, description, property number, unit of measure and other relevant information relative to the property and prepare a separate report for its unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property. OIC-Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation for all depreciable PPE. The Municipal Engineer was able to submit some details regarding the date when the infrastructure assets became operational.
observations.reason: The RPCPPE remains incomplete with some information. The details on the date when the infrastructure assets became operational are still for completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the list of delinquent taxpayers indicating the amount of collectible RPT/SET as at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was able to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the copy of the list of delinquent taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Assessor to: ● continue the processing of the titling of the purchased parcels of land; ● submit to the Municipal Accountant the SB resolution authorizing the transfer and other supporting documents; and ● resolve the issues with the heirs of the donor of the school site and the unlawful occupants of the plaza site. We also recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the OIC-Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer, to determine and record the fair values of the seven parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor was able to register title to one parcel of land. The other parcels of land were claimed by the private owners though court decision, hence dropped from the books. The SB resolution was submitted to the Municipal Accountant. The parcels of land were claimed by the private owners through a court decision. The Municipal Accountant recorded the fair value of the land in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Agriculturist to file to the Audit Team for evaluation the request for relief from accountability for the reported deaths or loss of dispersed animals together with the necessary supporting documents and adjust the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The request for relief from accountability for the reported deaths or loss of dispersed animals was submitted to the Audit Team for evaluation, contrary to Section 73 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, thus the appropriate audit decision and amounting to P25,000.00 was adjusted in the books of accounts based on the prior year’s records and audit results.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit and re-evaluate the available documents, look for alternative requirements in case of irretrievable documents and file with the Audit Team for evaluation the request for authority to write-off dormant and uncollectible receivables totaling P400,410.41. We also recommended to require the Municipal Treasurer to demand from the employee who is still in the active service the amount of P2,250.00.
observations.managementAction: Death certificate from one of the accountable officers was retrieved to support the request for authority to write-off the uncollectible receivables. The Municipal Treasurer was able to collect the amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445, subject to the authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that a claim arises in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF the P37,466.60.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant transfer the unutilized balance of funds held in trust by the Municipality to the GF totaling P119,972.30 and make a request to the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an Appropriation Ordinance for the use of the unutilized funds for COVID-19 related PPAs in accordance with the aforementioned guidelines. We also recommended to return immediately the amount of P11,810.00 to BFAR.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant transferred the unutilized balance of funds held in trust by the Municipality to the GF totaling P119,972.30 and made the necessary adjustments. P24,310.00 was returned to its source agency. P75,458.32 were adjusted to the books of accounts as these were later found to be errors in recording.
observations.reason: There are still unexpended balances subject for return to the National Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit promptly and intact all her collections to avoid accumulation of undeposited collections.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer deposited her collections promptly and intact.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to report the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF monthly, certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, and the MDRRMO-Designate to submit the same to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF were submitted to the Audit Team on a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the SWM Board provide the needed resources and assistance to expedite the completion and submission to the NSWMC of the 10-Year SWM Plan of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Municipality has proposed a new 10-Year SWM Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Assessor to continue the processing of the (a) titling of the purchased parcels of land; (b) donation of the site to the DepEd
observations.managementAction: Moreover, we recommended that the OIC-Municipal Assessor determine whether the Municipality has actually no more legal claims against the private owners of the market site and take necessary actions on how to deal with the unlawful occupants of the plaza site.
observations.reason: Due to constraints in retrieving the necessary documents from the lot owners.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to re-file/submit the request for authority to write-off the dormant receivables together with the relevant documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 for evaluation by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Due to lack of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the efforts of the Municipality in implementing measures to
contain the spread of COVID-19 by providing basic services to the affected
constituents and timely distribution of cash assistance to the targeted
beneficiaries. We recommended to continue its efforts to undertake urgent and
appropriate disaster response aid and measures in a timely manner to curtail and
eliminate the threat of COVID-19.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairperson to submit
to the Audit Team for evaluation the written explanation/justification for not observing
strict compliance with the GPPB guidelines on mandatory posting of all emergency
procurements of the Municipality. Henceforth, comply with the existing rules and
regulations relative to this.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Treasurer/Inventory Committee and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
determine the cause of discrepancy of their records and take appropriate
action for the result of reconciliation;
• Municipal Treasurer/Inventory Committee to provide/complete/correct the
information in the RPCPPE such as the conditions, whereabouts, description,
property number, unit of measure and other relevant information relative to the
property and prepare a separate report for the unserviceable properties and
dispose the same following the guidelines provided under COA Circular No.
92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
• OIC-Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation for all depreciable PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the list of delinquent taxpayers
indicating the amount of collectible RPT/SET as at the end of the year.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
OIC-Municipal Assessor to:
• continue the processing of the titling of the purchased parcels of land;
• submit to the Municipal Accountant the SB resolution authorizing the transfer
and other supporting documents relevant to the donated school site, copy
furnish the Audit Team; and
• resolve the issues with the heirs of the donor of the market site and the
unlawful occupants of the plaza site.
We also recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of
the OIC-Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer, to determine and record
the fair values of the seven parcels of land.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Agriculturist to file to the Audit Team for evaluation the request for relief
from accountability for the reported deaths or loss of dispersed animals together with
the necessary supporting documents and adjust the books by the overstatement of
P25,000.00 based on the prior year’s records and audit results.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to submit and re-evaluate the available documents, look for alternative
requirements in case of irretrievable documents and file with the Audit Team for
evaluation the request for authority to write-off dormant and uncollectible
receivables totaling P400,410.41.

We also recommended to require the Municipal Treasurer to demand from the
employee who is still in the active service the amount of P2,250.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund pursuant to
the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445, subject to the authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan (SB) with a condition that appropriation shall be made
available in the event that a claim arises in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to transfer the unutilized balance of funds held in trust by the Municipality to the
GF totaling P119,972.30 and make a request to the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an
Appropriation Ordinance for the use of the unutilized funds for COVID-19
related PPAs in accordance with the aforementioned guidelines. We also
recommended to return immediately the amount of P11,810.00 to the Bureau of
Fisheries and Aquatic Resources.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
deposit promptly and intact all her collections to avoid accumulation of
undeposited collections.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Agriculturist (MA) to update the report on dispersed animals and exert more effort to support the adjustment that will be made by the OIC-Municipal Accountant. If all efforts fail, request from the SB for the derecognition of the account.
observations.managementAction: The MA submitted the request to the SB for the derecognition of the Biological Assets in the books of account.
observations.reason: The SB resolution was enacted only for the derecognition of the Biological Assets on January 18, 2021 and the request for relief from property accountability is still for filing to the Audit Team. (Reiterated in audit observation no. 6, Part II of this report)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to revert the P961,131.88 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the SB with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that a claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Reverted the Payables to GF under JEV Nos. 2020-12-0015 and 2020-12-3398 dated December 28, 2020

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit promptly and intact all her collections and reconcile her cashbook with the accounting records at least quarterly to immediately adjust any unreconciled ledger and cashbook entries.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was made at the end of the year.
observations.reason: Due to non-reconciliation of records regularly, errors in the recording of collections and deposits were not adjusted immediately. (Reiterated in audit observation no. 10, Part II of this report)

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year at the beginning of the year; and inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the appropriate project that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted already the required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MPDC instruct the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to work together in the implementation of the programmed PPAs for 20 percent DF to ensure timely execution and fully achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P16,448,762.15 or 95.80 percent of current year’s appropriation was obligated as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and the SWM Board provide the needed resources and assistance to expedite the completion and submission of the 10-Year SWMP to the NSWMC to ensure that the programs and activities for SWM are consistent with the NSWMF which aims to protect public health and the environment.
observations.managementAction: Sought the assistance of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) on the formulation of the SWMP.
observations.reason: Due to other prioritized activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Assessor to facilitate the: • titling of the parcels of land to ensure the ownership of the Municipality over these lands; and • transfer of the school site to the DepEd if this is to be donated and derecognition of the market site valued at P315,000.00, provided there is an Sangguniang Bayan (SB) resolution authorizing the transfer and derecognition of the lands.
observations.managementAction: Inquired from the Registry of Deeds (RD) and other retrieving the necessary records
observations.reason: Due to constraints in retrieving the necessary records from the lot owners. (Reiterated under Part II, No. 1 of this report.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist (MA) to complete the verification/ investigation to establish the status of the recipients, dispersed animals and gather the necessary documents to support the adjustment that will be made by the OIC-Municipal Accountant. If all efforts fail, request from the SB for the derecognition of the account.
observations.managementAction: The MA continued the investigation but no new data were gathered by the former MA and Livestock Technician, thus the request for derecognition was submitted to the SB together with a copy of his investigation report, for resolution.
observations.reason: Records and documents were not available. (Reiterated under Part II, No. 2 of this report.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the relevant documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 for evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still gathering documents to support the write-off of the account. (Reiterated under Part II, No. 4 of this report.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MENRO-Designate, in coordination with the Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Board (MESWMB) expedite the preparation of the revised 10-Year SWMP for submission and approval of the NSWMC to ensure that the programs and activities for SWM are based on the National Solid Waste Management Framework which aims to protect the public health and the environment.
observations.managementAction: The Management is in the process of completing the 10-year SWMP.
observations.reason: Management is in the process of completing the 10-year SWMP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Assessor
to continue the processing of the (a) titling of the purchased parcels of land to
ensure the ownership of the Municipality, and (b) donation of the school site to
the DepEd provided it is supported with an SB resolution authorizing the transfer.
Moreover, we recommended that the OIC-Municipal Assessor determine whether
the Municipality has actually no more legal claims against the private owners of
the market site and take necessary actions on how to deal with the unlawful
occupants of the plaza site.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
OIC-Municipal Agriculturist to update the report on dispersed animals and exert
more effort to complete the necessary documents to support the adjustment that
will be made by the Municipal Accountant. If all efforts fail, request from the SB
for the derecognition of the account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to revert the P961,131.88 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund,
subject to an authority from the SB through a Resolution with a condition that
appropriation shall be made in the event that a claim arises in the future pursuant
to the provisions of Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
OIC-Municipal Accountant to re-file/submit the request for authority to write-off
dormant receivables together with the relevant documents required under COA
Circular No. 2016-005 for evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
deposit promptly and intact all her collections and reconcile her cashbook with
the accounting records at least quarterly to adjust immediately any discrepancies.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Engineer to submit the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to
be implemented during the year at the beginning of the year; and inform the Audit
Team within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the
start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public
notices are already posted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reverse P900,000.00 without supporting documents, thus the accounting entry made for the recognition of Accounts Payable of P900,000.00 and revert the P30,863.83 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the SB through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality have adjusted the amount of P900,000.00 to the general fund under JEV No. 8498 dated June 30, 2019. The request for authority to revert the payables to the GF was submitted to the SB Secretary on June 1, 2020.
observations.reason: Not implemented due to lack of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile her cashbook with the ledger of the Municipal Accountant regularly or at least quarterly to immediately correct any errors in recording, if any.
observations.managementAction: All discrepancies were recorded and deposited on January 4, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year at the beginning of the year; and inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer was not able to submit the documents within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Insufficient workforce.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to return the amount of P75,408.11 to the National Treasury, in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P75,408.11 was remitted to the National Treasury on June 21, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to work together in the implementation of the planned projects and activities for 20 percent DF to ensure timely implementation so that the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes are fully achieved.
observations.managementAction: Implemented the CY 2018 projects during the year.
observations.reason: The CY 2018 projects were not implemented timely.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRM Officer to maximize the utilization of LDRRMF by proper planning and full implementation of the LDRRMIP as mandated by law to lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has maximized the utilization of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Inquired from the Registry of Deeds (RD)/other Municipality necessary retrieving the relevant records of some parcels of land.
observations.reason: Due to constraints in retrieving the relevant records/documents from the lot owners.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to support the request for write-off with documents enumerated under COA Circular No. 2016-005 for evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still gathering documents to support the write-off of the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to confirm with the contractor and to request him to submit documents pertaining to his claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the SB through a Resolution with the condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant did not prioritize the reversion of the outstanding payables due to lack of time and manpower.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to
facilitate the (a) titling of the parcels of land to ensure the ownership of the
Municipality over these lands and (b) transfer of the school site to the DepEd if
this is to be donated and derecognition of the market site valued at P315,000.00,
provided there is an SB resolution authorizing the transfer and derecognition of
the lands.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to
complete the verification/investigation to establish the status of the dispersed
animals and gather the necessary documents to support the adjustment that will
be made by the OIC-Municipal Accountant. If all efforts fail, request from the
Sangguniang Bayan for the derecognition of the account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
reverse the accounting entry made for the recognition of the Accounts Payable of
P900,000.00 and revert the P30,863.83 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the
General Fund, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) through a
Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that
claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of P.D. No.
1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to submit the relevant documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 for
evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
reconcile her cashbook with the ledger of the Municipal Accountant regularly or at
least quarterly to immediately correct errors in recording, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to
submit the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during
the year at the beginning of the year; and inform the Audit Team within 10 days
after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are
already posted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in tracing the documents pertaining to the PPE Nos. 2100, 2101 and 2102 dated June 30, 2018 to establish its validity; record the and adjustment of PPEs. • Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records regularly with the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGU, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV tracing the documents pertaining to the PPE Nos. 2100, 2101 and 2102 dated June 30, 2018 to establish its validity; record the and adjustment of PPEs.
The Municipal Treasurer reconcile her Accounting Office records with the Municipal Accountant regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to: • pursue the collection of the amount of P2,500.00 from the employee who is still in service, otherwise deduct it from her salary or from whatever money due her; • send demand letter to the separated employee for the amount of P500.00; and • send collection notice to the heirs of the employee who is already deceased, if the efforts proved futile, prepare and submit to the SB a request for write off, duly supported with complete and proper documents.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P2,500.00 was already collected by the Municipality on January 5, 2019 under OR No. 757250. The Municipality collected a partial amount of P500.00 on January 22, 2019. The Municipal Accountant is gathering the supporting documents to support the write-off of the receivables. There is no assurance from the heirs that the loan will still be paid.
observations.reason: The balance is expected to be collected before the end of 2019; there is no assurance from the heirs that the remaining loan will be paid.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Agriculturist and the Municipal Treasurer to: • conduct investigation on the circumstances surrounding the death of the animals and determine if negligence was committed so that the concerned recipients may be compelled to refund the cost of the animals as part of their responsibility, pursuant to the Animal Dispersal Contract; • submit timely Livestock Death Certificate to the Municipal Accountant to support the dropping from the books the cost of dead animals in accordance with the rules and regulations on the relief from accountability; and • conduct a more intensive monitoring and evaluation of the project implementation to determine the success or failure of the program for further decision making. We further recommended to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments of the Biological Assets – Work/Zoo Animals account and other affected accounts once the reports are received from the OIC-Municipal Agriculturist for fair presentation of balances of the accounts in the Financial Statements (FS).
observations.managementAction: Many beneficiaries no longer live in the Municipality or are deceased. The Municipal Agriculturist conducted the investigation on the Animal Dispersal Project.
observations.reason: Records and documents were not available; lack of basis in recording the adjustment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to revert the amount of P80,660.24 to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer remitted a total amount of P5,252.14 in CY 2018.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer is still processing the return of remaining unexpended balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least 1 percent of their IRA for LCPC as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.
observations.managementAction: The appropriated 1 percent of the IRA in CY 2018 for LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM fund within 15th days after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO submitted the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM quarterly instead of monthly.
observations.reason: The reports submitted for CY 2018 were quarterly instead of monthly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent DF so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Appropriated the 20 percent to various development projects and programs.
observations.reason: The allotted amounts for the barangay projects were insufficient for the proposed projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to revert the unexpended balance of P132,975.74 immediately to the National Treasury pursuant to Supreme Court Decision under G.R. No. 208566 dated November 19, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P132,975.74 was reverted to the National Treasury on August 24, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

* Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in tracing the documents pertaining to
the PPE amounting to £208,835.14 to establish its validity; and

* Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records regularly with the Accounting
Office pursuant to Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGU, Volume L.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the subject lots to avoid contingent
claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA
Circular No. 92-386.

Accountant to support the request for write-off with documents enumerated
under COA Circular No, 2016-005 for evaluation of the Audit Team.

Treasurer to:

* pursue the collection of the amount of £2,500.00 from the employee who is
still in service, otherwise deduct it from her salary or from whatever money
due her;

* send demand letter to the separated employee for the payment of £2,750.00;
and

* send collection notice to the heirs of the employee who is already deceased, if
the efforts proved futile, prepare and submit to the SB a request for write off,
duly supported with complete and proper documents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
OIC-Municipal Agriculturist and the Municipal Treasurer to:

¢ conduct investigation on the circumstances surrounding the death of the
animals and determine if negligence was committed so that the concerned
recipients may be compelled to refund the cost of animals as part of their

¢ submit timely Livestock Death Certificate to the Municipal Accountant to
support the dropping from the books the cost of dead animals in accordance
with the rules and regulations on the relief from accountability; and

* conduct a more intensive monitoring and evaluation of the project
implementation to determine the success or failure of the program for further
decision making.

‘We further recommended to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the
necessary adjustments of the Biological Assets — Work/Zoo Animals account and
other affected accounts once the reports are received from the OIC-Municipal
Agriculturist for fair presentation of balances of the accounts in the Financial
Statements (FS),

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to confirm with the contractor and to request him to submit
documents pertaining to his claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated
Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the SB through a Resolution with
the condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the
future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445,

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to revert the amount of P80,660.24 to the National Treasury.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget
Officer to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC as required under DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

‘We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRM Officer
to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM fund within 15" days
after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to maximize the utilization of
the 20% DF so that all its programmed development projects could be
implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries avail of the
maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of subject lots to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.



Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor conduct investigation to determine the officials who failed to perform their responsibilities resulting in the loss of property and held them liable for the loss thereof
observations.reason:
The Municipal Assessor explained that the purchased parcels of land is programmed to be surveyed while the donated parcels of land were not acted by the Municipality due to the absence of necessary documents such as the Deed of Donation to substantiate management ownership.

The Municipal Mayor commented that the land amounting to P315,000.00 was donated in CY 1939 and there were no supporting documents such as deed of donation recovered. Moreover, he added that there is no loss to the government because there is no outflow of cash.

Further, the Municipal Mayor mentioned that he will request for an SB resolution for the derecognition of the land in the books of the Municipality, since the land was already named to a private person and there is no documents to support the records.

The lot is already titled in the name of private persons (the donor heirs). The Municipal Assessor added that it is no longer within the control of the Municipality.

See revised finding under Part II no. 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist and the Municipal Treasurer to:

conduct investigation on the circumstances surrounding the death of the animals and determine if negligence was committed so that the concerned recipients may be compelled to refund the cost of animals as part of their responsibility, pursuant to the Animal Dispersal Contract;

submit timely Livestock Death Certificate to the Municipal Accountant to support the dropping from the books the cost of dead animals in accordance with the rules and regulations on the relief from accountability; and

conduct a more intensive monitoring and evaluation of the project implementation to determine the success or failure of the program for further decision making.

We further recommended to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments of the Other Property, Plant and Equipment – Work/Zoo Animals account and other affected accounts once the reports are received from the Municipal Agriculturist for fair presentation of balances of the accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Mayor explained that the dispersal program was not during his term as well as the Municipal Agriculturist but promised to conduct an investigation to comply with the recommendation.

Failure of the Municipal Agriculturist to conduct investigation on the circumstances surrounding the death of the animals as he prioritized the activities in his office.  

Same as the reason above.

No documents to effect the adjustment in the books.  

Reiterated under Part II, Observation No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee to consolidate the items of similar nature in the PPMP to APP so that splitting may be avoided and to adopt the public bidding as the primary mode of procurement to promote transparency and equal opportunity among qualified suppliers and in accordance with the provisions provided under the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.reason:
The Municipality still procure materials as the need arises and were not in accordance with the APP.

Reiterated under Part II, Observation No. 10 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Report for DRRM Funds in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-009 dated August 28, 2014, to enable the Team to conduct audit of DRRM funds.
observations.reason:
The LDRRMO was not well informed about the submission of utilization report within the 15th day after the end of each month. 

Reiterated under Part II, Observation No. 09 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the supporting schedule for the Other PPE account amounting to P154,069.76 to establish its validity;
Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records regularly with the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 114 of the MNGAS, Volume I; and
Municipal Agriculturist and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct investigation on the circumstances surrounding the death of the animals and determine if negligence was committed so that the concerned recipients may be compelled to refund the cost of animals as part of their responsibility, pursuant to the Animal Dispersal Contract; submit timely Livestock Death Certificate to the Municipal Accountant to support the dropping from the books the cost of dead animals in accordance with the rules and regulations on the relief from accountability; and conduct a more intensive monitoring and evaluation of the project implementation to determine the success or failure of the program for further decision making.
We further recommended to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments of the Other PPE – Work/Zoo Animals account and other affected accounts once the reports are received from the Municipal Agriculturist for fair presentation of balances of the accounts in the Financial Statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to record all the lots owned by the Municipality; and
Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the subject lots to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor conduct investigation to determine the officials who failed to perform their responsibilities resulting in the loss of property and held them liable for the loss thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for CY 2016.
require the Municipal Accountant to establish at the beginning of the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Tax Payer’s Index Card the RPT/SET Receivables.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to support the request for write-off with documents enumerated under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC and provide programs, projects and activities for the promotion of the rights and welfare of children as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under the above-cited regulation to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and
instruct the Municipal Engineer/Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the list of all on-going PPA and those that are to be implemented during the year at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the offices concerned, establish proper coordination in the implementation and monitoring of the projects under the 20% DF in order to enhance the rate of implementation and ensure that benefits are delivered to the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format as provided in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and the LDRRMO to submit the said report to the COA Auditor for monitoring and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adopt the public bidding as the primary mode of procurement to promote transparency and equal opportunity among qualified suppliers and to avail of the most advantageous prices available in accordance with the provisions provided under the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant to adjust the Due to Other NGAs account pertaining to PDAF-Senators and PDAF-Cong. Suarez to reflect the corrected balance; and
Municipal Treasurer to deposit the total amount of P421,739.50 immediately to the account of the National Treasury pursuant to Supreme Court Decision under G.R. No. 208566 dated November 19, 2013.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records regularly to establish validity of the total PPEs of P14,460,500.61;



Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the supporting schedule for the PPEs amounting to P154,069.76 to establish its validity; and

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain complete and updated PPELC and Property Cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction:
Reconciliation of records were made by the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer.  However, Work/Zoo Animals account and Other PPE account were not yet reconciled.

The Municipal Accountant did not submit the supporting schedule to the Audit Team.


The OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer prepared the PPELC and Property Cards.
observations.reason:
Due to lack of documents.





Same as above.  Reiterated under Part II, Observation No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries by debiting the Due from GOCCs account and crediting the Other Machinery and Equipment account to fairly present the financial statements.

request the Sangguniang Bayan for a resolution requesting the write-off of the Receivables and file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable account duly supported by documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 100-2017-06-603 to adjust the accounts
observations.reason: SB Resolution No. 2017-037 was made requesting write-off of accounts. However, COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 requires additional supporting documents to be attached to the said request for write-off.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes; and

require the Municipal Accountant to establish at the beginning of the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Tax Payer’s Index Card the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax (RPT/SET) Receivables.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer have already remitted the amount due and collectible for 2015.


The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 100-2017-02-602 and 200-2017-02-22 to set-up the beginning balances of RPT/SET receivables for the year.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts for the operation of the Municipality’s public utilities/economic enterprises in compliance with Section 105 of the Manual on NGAS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is now preparing and maintaining subsidiary ledgers for special accounts of the operation of Market, Cemetery and Water System.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Municipal Accountant to use the perpetual inventory method by recording as inventory all purchases of supplies and expenses upon proper issuance of the same and to compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method; and

Property Officer to make sure that every request for supplies by the requisitioning office is supported with RIS and to prepare the SSMI weekly to be submitted to the Accounting Office as basis in preparing the JEV to record the supplies and materials issued representing expense.
observations.managementAction:
The OIC-Municipal Accountant used perpetual inventory method in recording inventory purchases.



The Property Officer is now preparing RIS for every request for supplies by the requisitioning office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

submit the Disbursement Voucher for the grant of Confidential Fund cash advance with complete required supporting documents to the Audit Team 

require the Municipal Accountant to record the grant of cash advance for Confidential Fund under the Special Disbursing Officer account and the Confidential Fund Expenses account upon receipt of the transmittal letter duly received by the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit authorized staff and stop the practice of granting additional cash advance unless the previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor committed to submit all Confidential Fund DVs supported with complete documents to the Audit Team.


The Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents enumerated above for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have submitted to the Audit Team the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with the regulation on posting of signboards as soon as the award has been made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer ensure compliance with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit the:

list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year, as of January 1, 2015

monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations
observations.managementAction:
The list of all on-going PPA as of January 1, 2015 was submitted to the Audit Team.


Monthly monitoring report is already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point to comply with the Procedural Guidelines under Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM), National Economic Development Authority (NEDA), and National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women (NCRFW).
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal person have submitted the GAD plan and budget to the Provincial Planning and Development Office as required in JMC 2016-01 as amended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the total amount of P526,739.50 immediately to the account of the National Treasury pursuant to Supreme Court Decision under G.R. No. 208566 dated November 19, 2013.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer remitted the amount of P105.000.00 to the National Treasury. The remaining balance is yet to be remitted.
